$comment: Fixture provenance ledger — distinguishes back-authored fixtures (reconstructed from golden MDs in the catalog) from pipeline-produced fixtures (output of a live Phase 1-6 run). Required before Step G authors form.json from real gh api data. Codex's separate-file approach per board review docs/External-Board-Reviews/041826-step-g-kickoff/CONSOLIDATION.md §U-3/FX-4 (adopted 2-1 over schema-mutation; rationale: scan-schema.json describes live artifacts, not test-harness metadata). Keyed by fixture filename.

provenance_values.back-authored-from-golden-md: Fixture manually reconstructed from an existing catalog MD. Validates schema but does NOT prove what a live Phase 1-6 pipeline would produce. Useful for renderer testing; insufficient for 'pipeline reliable' claims.
provenance_values.authored-from-scan-data: Fixture authored during active scan work using real gh api output, but NOT the result of a fully automated pipeline run. Between back-authored and pipeline-produced.
provenance_values.pipeline-produced: Fixture output of a live Phase 1-6 scan against a fresh gh api capture. The form.json here was NOT hand-edited post-pipeline. Required provenance for any form.json promoted to the catalog as a V2.5-preview entry.
provenance_values.step-g-failed-artifact: Fixture produced during Step G that did NOT pass acceptance (validator error, parity mismatch, structural-parity failure vs V2.4 scan of same repo). Kept as test artifact, NOT as operator exemplar. Future operators must not mirror these.

zustand-form.json.authored_by: Claude Opus 4.7 operator + Sonnet 4.6 subagent (10 FA batch)
zustand-form.json.notes: Enriched from the V2.4 zustand golden via 10 board-approved fix artifacts. Raw data mostly from the actual scan; enrichments (executable_file_inventory, PR sample, verdict_exhibits, etc.) were structured from the golden MD.

caveman-form.json.authored_for: Step F cross-shape fixture coverage (curl-pipe-from-main installer shape)
caveman-form.json.notes: Back-authored reconstruction of the 692-line caveman golden. All 9 findings kept (F0, F5, F11, F14, F16, F-advisory, F-amplify, F-batch, F-deps). No subset scoping. Step F R3 fix commit ce698d4 removed a stale exhibit-tag F-ID reference (Distribution · F1+F2) that did not match the fixture's own finding set.

archon-subset-form.json.authored_for: Step F cross-shape fixture coverage (agentic platform monorepo shape + V1.1 C3 auto_load_tier constraint exercise)
archon-subset-form.json.subset_scope: 4 findings kept of 11 in golden — F0 (Critical, C20 governance), F1 (Warning, code/agent-rule-injection; triggers V1.1 C3 auto_load_tier constraint), F4 (Warning, governance review rate), F7 (OK, active security work). Dropped F2/F3/F5/F6/F8/F9/F10 as thematic variations of the same cluster. 4 kept cover severity spread across Critical/Warning/OK.
archon-subset-form.json.notes: First fixture to exercise the V1.1 C3 auto_load_tier constraint path. Monorepo data populated from golden (11 inner packages). Split-verdict by deployment (self-host vs multi-tenant). Step F R3 fix commit ce698d4 removed 2 stale exhibit-tag references (Supply chain · F2, Distribution · F8) pointing to dropped findings.

ordering_constraints.step_g_prerequisite: Any form.json produced by a live Step G run must be assigned a provenance value ('pipeline-produced' on acceptance, 'step-g-failed-artifact' on failure) BEFORE it is proposed for catalog promotion. Retroactive tagging defeats the purpose.
ordering_constraints.fixture_in_catalog: A fixture MUST carry provenance='pipeline-produced' to be referenced as an exemplar in scanner-catalog.md. Back-authored fixtures are test-only artifacts; they must never be held up as pipeline evidence.

references.schema: docs/scan-schema.json (V1.1 — not mutated for provenance per Codex R3 architectural position)
